text: The definition-of-done references the installed slash commands by name (e.g. /code-review returns APPROVE, /gate reports no blocking failures, /security-review) as completion criteria.
Points the executor at the owning skill (project-conventions / api-schema) by reference instead of copying those rules/schemas into the prompt.
Does not instruct the prompt to 'use agent X' / spawn a specific subagent — Claude Code delegates to subagents automatically, so naming one would be noise.
Names the quality gates (review, gate, security) as the bar to pass rather than pasting their internal configuration or re-deriving the checks.
References only capabilities actually listed as installed; does not invent commands/skills that were not in the inventory.
